data.content:
### Install @vercel/remote-nx Plugin

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

These commands show how to install the @vercel/remote-nx plugin using different package managers like pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun. This plugin is essential for enabling remote caching with Nx on Vercel.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/remote-nx
```

--------------------------------

### Clear Local Cache and Reinstall Dependencies

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

These commands demonstrate how to clear the local Nx cache and reinstall project dependencies using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). This is a common step after configuring caching to ensure a clean state.

```bash
pnpm i
```

```bash
yarn i
```

```bash
npm i
```

```bash
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/related-projects npm Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/index

Install the `@vercel/related-projects` package to easily access and use information about related projects in your deployments. This package simplifies fetching and parsing environment variables related to your project setup.

```bash
npm i @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
bun add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
pnpm add @vercel/related-projects
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Related Project Data with @vercel/related-projects

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/index

Fetch the related project data directly using the `relatedProjects` function from the `@vercel/related-projects` package. This function returns a fully typed array of `VercelRelatedProject` objects.

```typescript
import {
  relatedProjects,
  type VercelRelatedProject,
} from '@vercel/related-projects';

// fully typed project data
const projects: VercelRelatedProject[] = relatedProjects();
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Turborepo Caching with Environment Variables (JSON)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

This JSON configuration for `turbo.json` demonstrates how to manage environment variables to influence Turborepo's caching behavior. It specifies environment variables that impact build tasks and global tasks, as well as files whose content should affect all task hashes. This helps ensure that different environments or code changes correctly trigger cache invalidation. Requires Turborepo version 1.5+.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://turborepo.com/schema.json",
  "pipeline": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "env": [
        // env vars will impact hashes of all "build" tasks
        "SOME_ENV_VAR"
      ],
      "outputs": ["dist/**"]
    },
    "web#build": {
      // override settings for the "build" task for the "web" app
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "env": ["SOME_OTHER_ENV_VAR"],
      "outputs": [".next/**", "!.next/cache/**"]
    }
  },
  "globalEnv": [
    "GITHUB_TOKEN" // env var that will impact the hashes of all tasks,
  ],
  "globalDependencies": [
    "tsconfig.json" // file contents will impact the hashes of all tasks,
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Test Turborepo Remote Caching

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

Command to run a build after linking to the Vercel Remote Cache. Turborepo caches filesystem output locally and remotely. Running 'turbo run build' again after making changes will demonstrate the speed improvement due to only rebuilding changed files.

```bash
turbo run build
```

--------------------------------

### Link Turborepo to Vercel Remote Cache

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

Commands to link your Turborepo to the Vercel Remote Cache. This allows sharing build artifacts and computations across your team and CI/CD pipelines. These commands should be run from the root of your monorepo.

```bash
pnpm i 
turbo link
```

```bash
yarn i 
turbo link
```

```bash
npm i 
turbo link
```

```bash
bun i 
turbo link
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Nx Cache Directory in nx.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

This JSON snippet shows how to configure the cache directory for Nx tasks runner in the nx.json file. It specifies the runner and options, including the cache directory path, to enable local caching.

```json
{
  "tasksRunnerOptions": {
    "default": {
      "runner": "nx/tasks-runners/default",
      "options": {
        "cacheDirectory": "/tmp/nx-cache"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure @vercel/remote-nx Runner in nx.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

This JSON configuration updates the tasksRunnerOptions in nx.json to use the @vercel/remote-nx runner. It includes essential options like cacheableOperations, token, and teamId for Vercel remote caching.

```json
{
  "tasksRunnerOptions": {
    "default": {
      "runner": "@vercel/remote-nx",
      "options": {
        "cacheableOperations": ["build", "test", "lint", "e2e"],
        "token": "<token>",
        "teamId": "<teamId>"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Nx Runtime Cache Inputs for Environment Variables

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/monorepo-faq

Prevent Nx from using cached environment variable values by defining them as runtime hash inputs in nx.json. This ensures that the latest environment variable values are used during deployments.

```json
{
  "runtimeCacheInputs": [
    "echo $MY_VERCEL_ENV"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Reference Related Project Hosts with @vercel/related-projects

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/index

Use the `withRelatedProject` function from `@vercel/related-projects` to easily reference hosts of related projects. It allows specifying a default host if the related project data is missing or cannot be parsed.

```typescript
import {
  withRelatedProject
} from '@vercel/related-projects';

const apiHost = withRelatedProject({
  projectName: 'my-api-project',
  /**
   * Specify a default host that will be used for my-api-project if the related project
   * data cannot be parsed or is missing.
   */
  defaultHost: process.env.API_HOST,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Define Related Projects in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/index

Specify related projects for an app in its `vercel.json` file. This makes the preview and production hosts of the related project available as environment variables in the current deployment. Each app can list up to three related projects.

```json
{
  "relatedProjects": ["prj_123"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Turborepo Output Directories in turbo.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

This JSON configuration specifies the output directories for various frameworks within a Turborepo build pipeline. It ensures that Vercel can locate build artifacts after a cache hit. Aligning these outputs with your Framework Preset is crucial for successful deployments.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://turborepo.com/schema.json",
  "pipeline": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "outputs": [
        // Next.js
        ".next/**", "!.next/cache/**"
        // SvelteKit
        ".svelte-kit/**", ".vercel/**",
        // Build Output API
        ".vercel/output/**"
        // Other frameworks
        ".nuxt/**", "dist/**" "other-output-directory/**"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run Turborepo Build Command

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/turborepo

Execute the Turborepo build command directly when Turborepo is available globally. The appropriate filter is automatically inferred based on the root directory configuration. This avoids needing 'turbo' as a direct dependency in your application.

```bash
turbo build
```

--------------------------------

### Authenticate Turborepo CLI with Vercel Account

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/remote-caching

Authenticates the Turborepo CLI with your Vercel account. This step is required once your Vercel project is using Turborepo. It ensures secure access to Vercel services.

```pnpm
pnpm i 
```

```yarn
yarn i 
```

```npm
npm i 
```

```bun
bun i 
```

--------------------------------

### Proxying Requests with vercel.json Rewrites

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/monorepo-faq

Configure a main Vercel project to proxy requests to other upstream projects under a single domain using the rewrites property in vercel.json. This allows for hosting multiple projects on separate domains under one primary domain.

```json
{
  "rewrites": [
    { "source": "/project-a", "destination": "http://project-a.vercel.app" },
    { "source": "/project-b", "destination": "http://project-b.vercel.app" }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Vercel CLI Project Linking Override with Environment Variables

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/monorepo-faq

Use Vercel CLI without project linking by providing VERCEL_ORG_ID and VERCEL_PROJECT_ID environment variables. This is particularly useful for deployments from CI/CD providers.

```zsh
VERCEL_ORG_ID=team_123 VERCEL_PROJECT_ID=prj_456 vercel
```

=== COMPLETE CONTENT === This response contains all available snippets from this library. No additional content exists. Do not make further requests.
